_meta.description: Runtime metadata injected by bin/load-profile.sh after a profile is resolved. NOT present on profile files at rest — writing this field into a stored profile is meaningless because the loader overwrites it. Skill-layer callers read _meta to detect shadowing without scraping stderr.

source_path.description: Filesystem path of the resolved profile.

shadowed_starter.description: Path to a starter profile that this user/team profile shadowed (omit when nothing was shadowed).

shadowed_team.description: Paths to team profiles shadowed by this user profile.

extends_chain.$comment: v1.11.0 — ordered list of profile names from child to root when extends was used.
extends_chain.description: Profile name chain from child to root ancestor, e.g. ["my-react", "react-vite", "node-api"].

extends_resolved.$comment: v1.11.0 — true when extends was present and successfully resolved.
extends_resolved.description: True when the profile used extends and the chain was resolved.

extends.$comment: v1.11.0 — optional parent profile name to inherit from. Child fields overlay parent via deep merge; arrays replace (not concatenate). Max chain depth: 3. Workspace inline profiles cannot use extends. Supports namespaced team/name format.
extends.description: Name of a parent profile to inherit from. Resolution follows the same user > team > starter chain as load-profile.sh. May use namespace/name for team profiles.

schemaVersion.description: Profile schema version. Incremented on breaking changes.

archetype.$comment: v1.6.0 — codebase archetype. 'infra' covers IaC monorepos (Terraform/CDK/Pulumi/K8s/Helm). When set, overrides detect-stack.sh's auto-detected archetype during scaffolding. Drives archetype-aware Project Memory scaffolding when documentation.use_archetype_scaffolds is true. Optional.

stale_after_days.description: Age threshold (in days) for `bin/check-stale-branches.sh`. Local branches whose last commit is older than this AND that are not merged into a base branch are flagged as `stale_unmerged`. Doctor folds the result into its drift display. Default 90.

dependency_updater.description: Generate a dependency-updater config when bootstrapping or retrofitting (bin/gen-dependency-updater.sh). Three valid forms: `false` (off — current default; preserves backward-compat), `"dependabot"` (emit .github/dependabot.yml using the profile's stack to pick ecosystems), or `"renovate"` (emit renovate.json with config:recommended). Opt-in: starter profiles do NOT flip this on automatically — set it explicitly to opt your repo in.

devcontainer.description: When true, bin/gen-devcontainer.sh emits a .devcontainer/devcontainer.json keyed to the profile's primary_language. Default false (opt-in) so existing repos don't get a devcontainer materialised on the next bootstrap/retrofit without explicit consent. The generated file is preview-then-apply with idempotent re-runs; user edits survive on subsequent regenerations only when the rendered output happens to match byte-for-byte (otherwise the operator sees a diff and chooses overwrite vs. preserve).

ci.description: Optional CI workflow generation settings. Controls GitHub Actions workflow output.

github.description: GitHub branch / tag / repo-security settings. Used by `bin/gh-integration.sh` for both --apply (write protection) and --check (audit protection drift via the doctor probe).

require_pr_reviews.description: Number of approving PR reviews required to merge into a strategy-declared branch. 0 disables the review-required rule entirely (single-developer flow).

items.description: Status-check context name as it appears in GitHub's Branch protection screen. Matrix-job names include the matrix vector (e.g. 'lint + bats (ubuntu-latest)').

require_status_checks.description: Status checks that MUST pass before merging into a strategy-declared branch. Empty array means no required checks.

require_code_owner_reviews.description: When true, branch protection requires a review from a CODEOWNERS-listed reviewer. Only meaningful when a CODEOWNERS file exists; gh-integration.sh --check warns when this is true but no CODEOWNERS file ships in the repo, and warns when CODEOWNERS exists but the gate is off.

tag_protection_pattern.description: fnmatch-style tag pattern that should be protected against deletion + force-push (e.g. 'v*'). Audited via the GitHub Rulesets API; setting this enables the tag_protection section of `gh-integration.sh --check`. Absent = audit skipped.

require_signed_commits.description: When true, branch protection requires GPG/SSH-signed commits on strategy-declared branches. The audit also reads the local `commit.gpgsign` git config (warn when remote requires signing but local config is unset).

require_signed_tags.description: When true, the audit asserts the local `tag.gpgsign` config is on. There is no GitHub-side rule for required signed tags; this is purely a local-config audit so release.sh's `git tag` commands produce signed tags.

allow_squash_merge.description: When true, audit asserts the repo allows squash-merge from the PR merge button. Mirrors the GitHub repo setting `allow_squash_merge`.

allow_rebase_merge.description: When true, audit asserts the repo allows rebase-merge from the PR merge button. Mirrors `allow_rebase_merge`.

allow_merge_commit.description: When true, audit asserts the repo allows merge-commits from the PR merge button. Mirrors `allow_merge_commit`. Most projects want exactly one of squash / rebase / merge-commit enabled.

delete_branch_on_merge.description: When true, audit asserts the repo auto-deletes head branches after merge. Mirrors `delete_branch_on_merge`. Recommended for hygiene; pairs with the local cleanup-branches skill.

auto_merge_strategy.description: Merge strategy `bin/pr.sh --auto-merge` uses when calling `gh pr merge --auto`. Default `squash` when unset. The repo must allow the chosen strategy via .github.allow_*_merge for auto-merge to succeed; the audit catches that mismatch in the repo_settings section.

codeowners.$comment: v1.11.0 — explicit path → owner mappings for CODEOWNERS generation. Applies to both monorepo and single-repo profiles. Listed last in generated CODEOWNERS so they take precedence (GitHub uses last-match-wins).

governance.description: Optional CI governance gate configuration. Controls doctor-ci.sh behavior when run as a PR check.

enabled.description: When true, gen-ci --governance generates the governance-check workflow. Default true when the block is present.

threshold.description: Minimum health score (0-100) to pass the CI gate. Default 70.

severity.description: block = fail the check when score < threshold; warn = annotate but pass; off = skip entirely. Default 'block'.

ignore.description: Health-score categories to exclude from the CI gate score. Useful for suppressing noisy checks (e.g. orphans, stale) in early adoption.

release.description: Optional release-skill configuration. When absent, release skill defaults to conventional-changelog against CHANGELOG.md.

changelog_path.description: Relative path (from repo root) where CHANGELOG entries are appended. Must not start with '-' (option injection) or contain '..' (path traversal).

tag_prefix.description: Prefix for version tags (default: 'v'). Must not start with '-' (option injection) or contain shell metacharacters.

bump_files.description: Files to mutate in-place during release.sh --bump-manifests. Each entry declares a path + format + format-specific args. Bumps land in the release commit alongside CHANGELOG.md.

path.description: Relative path from repo root. Must not start with '-' (option injection); leading '.' is permitted so dotfile-prefixed paths like '.claude-plugin/plugin.json' work. The `not` clause additionally rejects any segment that is exactly '.' or '..' (catches '../foo', './foo', 'foo/../bar', '..', and '.' alone), making path-traversal a schema-layer error rather than relying on the bin/release.sh runtime check alone.

format.description: How to mutate the file. json-version-key uses jq; toml-version-key sed-edits a single-line `version = "x.y.z"` within a section; script invokes a user-provided command with $NEW_VERSION.

key.description: json-version-key: jq path to the version field (e.g. '.version' or '.plugins[0].version'). REQUIRED for json-version-key. Restricted to dotted-identifier + numeric-array-index forms so a profile can't smuggle a multi-step jq pipeline (e.g. `. | env`) through the bumper. Any character not in [A-Za-z0-9._\[\]] is rejected.

section.description: toml-version-key: TOML section header WITHOUT brackets (e.g. 'project' or 'package'). The first single-line `version = "..."` after `[<section>]` is rewritten. REQUIRED for toml-version-key.

command.description: script: shell command run with $NEW_VERSION exported. The command is responsible for mutating the file at .path. REQUIRED for script.

workspaces.description: Per-workspace config overrides for monorepos. Keys are relative paths (e.g. 'apps/web', 'packages/api') or '*' for all unmatched workspaces. Merged with detected workspace stacks by bin/resolve-workspace-configs.sh.

propertyNames.description: Either '*' for the catch-all override or a repo-relative path. Path constraint matches base_branches[]'s shape so workspace keys can't smuggle leading-dash or shell-metachar values into resolve-workspace-configs.sh.

profile.description: Name of a profile to apply to this workspace (e.g. 'flutter-app', 'node-api'). When set, resolve-workspace-configs.sh loads the full profile and merges its hooks/extras/ci into this workspace's config. If absent, the workspace inherits the root profile.

ci.description: Per-workspace CI overrides applied on top of the workspace's resolved profile (named or root). Booleans toggle individual phases; version fields override the matrix runtime.

documentation.description: Per-workspace documentation scaffolding override. When present, the workspace's doc scaffolds are driven by this subset of fields (merged onto the named-profile or root documentation block).

use_archetype_scaffolds.$comment: v1.6.0 — opt-in flag for archetype-aware Project Memory scaffolding. When true, scaffold_types is replaced by the per-archetype map at scaffold time (api-service → architecture/api-reference/runbook/deployment/adrs/glossary, cli-tool → architecture/runbook/adrs/glossary, etc.). When false or absent, the flat scaffold_types list is used (pre-v1.6.0 behavior).

staleness_threshold_commits.description: Minimum number of correlated source commits to flag a doc as stale. Default 5 when omitted.

staleness_threshold_days.description: Fallback days threshold for staleness detection. Default 30 when omitted.

drift_check.description: Public-doc governance configuration. Each detector can be toggled independently; count_claims requires explicit tracked_counts to be useful.

readme_badges.description: README shields.io badge generator. Master `enabled` plus per-badge sub-flags.

glossary.$comment: v1.7.0 — glossary auto-population. When auto_populate is true AND scaffold_types includes glossary (or archetype scaffolds emit it), bin/scaffold-glossary.sh seeds docs/glossary.md from detected exported types in the codebase. The detected block lives between <!-- nyann:glossary:auto-start --> and <!-- nyann:glossary:auto-end --> markers and is regenerated idempotently; user content outside the markers is preserved.

auto_populate.description: Opt-in. Default false to preserve v1.6.0 behaviour.

max_terms.description: Cap on auto-detected terms. Detector picks the top N by external reference count so the glossary stays bounded per the size-budgeted Project Memory property.

languages.description: Restrict detection to specific languages. "auto" lets the detector use the StackDescriptor's primary + secondary languages.

guards.description: Pre-action guard configuration. Per-flow arrays declare which guards to run; profiles may promote (advisory → confirm/critical) but never silently demote. Omitting a flow means 'run all built-in guards at their built-in severity'.

iac.$comment: v1.13.0 — optional Infrastructure-as-Code pinning block. Mirrors the stack-descriptor iac shape so a profile can pin the tool and declare which repo-relative paths are deployable units when auto-discovery (detect-iac.sh / discover-iac-units.sh) is ambiguous. Additive and fully optional: absent on non-infra profiles; presence overrides nothing for repos that ignore it.
iac.description: Infrastructure-as-Code overrides. Pins the IaC tool and/or declares deployable units so per-unit versioning, drift, and doc routing key off real paths instead of guessing.

units.description: Deployable/versionable units declared by the operator. Each maps a kind + repo-relative path to a logical name; version is an optional pin.

path.description: Repo-relative path to the unit (e.g. 'modules/vpc'). Must not start with '-' (option injection); the `not` clause rejects '.'/'..' path segments (traversal), matching release.bump_files[].path.

name.description: Lowercase-kebab logical identifier for the unit. Same shape as the top-level profile `name`.

version.description: Optional pinned version of the unit (e.g. a Helm chart version or module ref). Free-form within a safe charset.

drift_check.$comment: v1.13.0 (I8) — IaC drift governance. Read by bin/iac-drift-scan.sh, doctor's iac-drift probe, and the unpinned-iac-refs/committed-secrets guards. Flat-boolean per-detector toggles (mirroring documentation.readme_badges, and matching the spec's flag names verbatim). Master `enabled` short-circuits the whole subsystem. All additive and default-on; absent block = all detectors run.
drift_check.description: IaC drift governance. Master `enabled` plus per-detector boolean toggles. Read by bin/iac-drift-scan.sh.

enabled.description: Master switch. false short-circuits the iac-drift scan to an empty report.

unpinned_refs.description: Toggle the unpinned-refs detector (moving module refs, unpinned providers/deps). Severity: high.

missing_lockfile.description: Toggle the missing-lockfile detector (.terraform.lock.hcl / Chart.lock / Pulumi plugins). Severity: medium.

secrets_in_vars.description: Toggle the secrets-in-vars detector (committed credentials in var files). Severity: critical.

version_lag.description: Toggle the version-lag detector (Helm appVersion behind the latest git tag). Severity: medium.

scanned_files.description: Optional explicit scan set (repo-relative paths). Overrides the default multi-pattern find when present.

name.description: Guard identifier (e.g. 'staged-files-exist'). Matches a script under bin/guards/<name>.sh.

severity.description: Override the built-in severity. Profiles may promote advisory guards to confirm or critical.
